identity.short_name: Coefficient lag autocorrelation via Fourier coefficient and cosine moments

problem_spec.summary: Compute a fixed-lag coefficient autocorrelation of a structured polynomial by rewriting the target as a Laurent coefficient of P(x)P(x^{-1}), converting it to a unit-circle Fourier integral via orthogonality, simplifying |P(e^{iθ})|^2 to a polynomial in cos(2θ), and evaluating the resulting integral using even cosine moments (central binomial coefficients).
givens: a_k is the coefficient of x^k in P(x).
C = Σ_{k} a_k a_{k+4}, summing over k such that k+4 \leq deg(P).
ask: Determine the exact integer value of C.

parameter(s).name: exponent
parameter(s).purpose: controls the power on each quadratic factor, hence the degree and sum length

parameter(s).name: linear_coefficient_u
parameter(s).purpose: sets the conjugate-like quadratic pair (x^2+u x+1),(x^2-u x+1) enabling cosine reduction

parameter(s).name: lag_t
parameter(s).purpose: specifies which coefficient correlation Σ a_k a_{k+t} is extracted as a Fourier coefficient

invariant(s).name: correlation_is_fourier_coefficient
invariant(s).statement: If P(x)=Σ a_k x^k, then Σ_k a_k a_{k+t} = [x^t] P(x)P(x^{-1}) = (1/2\pi)∫_0^{2\pi} |P(e^{iθ})|^2 e^{-itθ} dθ. For real coefficients, this equals (1/2\pi)∫ |P(e^{iθ})|^2 cos(tθ) dθ.

essential_assumption(s): P has real coefficients so P(e^{-iθ}) is the complex conjugate of P(e^{iθ}).
Orthogonality of exponentials on [0,2\pi] is used to identify coefficients via integrals.
Even-power cosine moment formula applies: (1/2\pi)∫_0^{2\pi} cos^{2j}(θ) dθ = C(2j,j)/4^j.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The polynomial P is fixed by the givens, so the coefficient sequence (a_k) is unique. The target C is the coefficient [x^t] of the uniquely determined Laurent polynomial P(x)P(x^{-1}), equivalently a unique Fourier coefficient of the uniquely determined function |P(e^{iθ})|^2. Therefore C is uniquely determined.

technique(s).name: constant_term_identity_for_shifted_correlation
technique(s).purpose: rewrite Σ a_k a_{k+t} as the coefficient [x^t] of P(x)P(x^{-1})

technique(s).name: Fourier_orthogonality_to_integral
technique(s).purpose: convert coefficient extraction into a unit-circle integral with e^{-itθ}

technique(s).name: unit_circle_trig_simplification_for_conjugate_quadratics
technique(s).purpose: simplify |P(e^{iθ})|^2 to a polynomial in cos(2θ)

technique(s).name: cosine_moment_evaluation_with_extra_cos_factor
inputs: cos(tθ) factor (for even t, reduced using angle-doubling identities)
technique(s).purpose: expand and evaluate using even-power cosine integrals, discarding odd moments

theorem(s).name: Orthogonality_of_complex_exponentials
theorem(s).role: identifies Laurent coefficients as Fourier coefficients via (1/2\pi)∫ e^{i(k-\ell)θ} dθ

theorem(s).name: Parseval_constant_term_principle
theorem(s).role: relates coefficient correlations to integrals of |P(e^{iθ})|^2 against e^{-itθ}

theorem(s).name: double_angle_and_power_reduction_identities
theorem(s).role: reduce cos(tθ) (t even) to a polynomial in cos(2θ) to match the integrand form

critical_trick(s): Replace Σ a_k a_{k+t} by the coefficient [x^t] of P(x)P(x^{-1}) and then by a Fourier integral.
Simplify |P(e^{iθ})|^2 using conjugate-quadratic factorization to a function of cos(2θ).
Reduce cos(tθ) to a polynomial in cos(2θ) and evaluate via even cosine moments.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: exact symbolic expansion and coefficient extraction for audit

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: exact integer binomial coefficients via comb for verifying moment sums

task(s).name: verify_correlation_by_expansion
task(s).reason: expand P(x), compute coefficients a_k, and verify Σ a_k a_{k+4}

task(s).name: verify_moment_sum
task(s).reason: compute the derived binomial/moment expression and confirm it matches the coefficient result
